pi_items.source: HTTP request verification - curl -I returned HTTP/2 200, site is publicly accessible and contains expected test pages
Task description indicates CSV export for X-Robots-Tag data; filename is reasonable based on content type

Matches directory pattern used in other Screaming Frog SEO Spider tasks in the same environment (canonical_tag_audit uses ~/Documents/SEO/exports/); confirmed in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh scripts

HTTP header verification - confirmed X-Robots-Tag header exists on test pages (e.g., https://crawler-test.com/robots_protocol/x_robots_tag_noindex returns 'x-robots-tag: noindex' in response headers); Screaming Frog documentation confirms it detects and exports X-Robots-Tag headers

Comprehensive link extraction from crawler-test.com homepage (extracted all 428 href attributes); searched for '/headers/x_robots_tag_' pattern - found zero results. Actual X-Robots-Tag test pages are located at '/robots_protocol/x_robots_tag_noindex', '/robots_protocol/x_robots_multiple_directives', and '/robots_protocol/meta_robots_and_x_robots_conflict'. No '/headers/' directory containing X-Robots-Tag pages exists on the site.

privileged_info_summary: crawler-test.com is a real, publicly accessible SEO testing website with pages containing X-Robots-Tag HTTP headers. Screaming Frog SEO Spider is a real, documented web crawling tool that can detect, capture, and export X-Robots-Tag headers from crawled pages via its Directives tab. However, the expected_url_part metadata contains an incorrect path: the task claims pages will be found at '/headers/x_robots_tag_' but the actual X-Robots-Tag test pages on crawler-test.com are located at '/robots_protocol/x_robots_tag_*' (e.g., /robots_protocol/x_robots_tag_noindex). This discrepancy would cause agents to look for URLs that do not exist on the target domain.
